mode_notes.lite: Do not create subagent agents in OpenClaw; subagent responsibilities are executed inline by the link body.
mode_notes.full: Create all subagent agents in OpenClaw.

mission_title: Consolidate knowledge handoff, terminology index, resource arbitration rules, and README examples to form an OpenClaw collaboration layer.

ontology.link_body: A collaboration unit consisting of one conductor and multiple subagents; lite mode does not create subagent agents in OpenClaw.
ontology.link_conductor: Coordination hub inside a link body
ontology.subagent: Individual agent responsible for a single scope

primary_reasons: Knowledge Link Body has the highest overall score: 13.
Matched keywords: knowledge, consolidation, terminology, index, rules.
Matched high-priority keywords: knowledge, consolidation, terminology, index.
support_reasons: Trigger support link body Rationality: primary link body is not Rationality Link Body.
Trigger support link body Validation: primary link body is not Validation Link Body; test context exists.
Trigger support link body Documentation: primary link body is not Documentation Link Body; task hits keywords documentation, readme, instructions, tutorial, examples, handbook, migration.
Security Link Body enters the support chain with score 2 to cover dimensions not in the primary chain.

knowledge_handoff.summary: Produce a reusable knowledge handoff for the mission "Consolidate knowledge handoff, terminology index, resource arbitration rules, and README examples to form an OpenClaw collaboration layer" to support the next iteration.
carry_forward: Record the Knowledge Link Body deliverables, boundaries, and member responsibilities.
Archive evidence summaries, risk lists, and confidence conclusions to avoid repeat judgment.
Preserve key path index: README.en.md, exmachina-en/README.md, exmachina-en/runtime/README.md, PROMPT.en.md, exmachina-en/BOOTSTRAP.md.
Consolidate user-facing instructions, examples, and migration differences.
Consolidate security assumptions, threat surfaces, hardening advice, and items needing review.
Consolidate the terminology list, decision index, question index, and reuse entry points.
decisions: Primary link body selection: Knowledge Link Body (score 13).
Support chain: Rationality Link Body, Validation Link Body, Documentation Link Body, Security Link Body.
Workspace stack: JSON, Markdown, Shell, TOML.
Preserve evidence levels, rejected options, and arbitration rationale for key conclusions.
open_questions: Record unverified assumptions from this round separately to avoid being treated as confirmed next round.
Convert high-risk but unexecuted actions into explicit to-dos and verification questions.
Continue tracking unconfirmed vulnerability assumptions, permission boundaries, and sensitive configuration risks.
next_updates: Sync new structure conventions, process rules, or examples back into README and export notes.
Update long-term docs with new critical paths, commands, or configuration entry points from this round.
Re-verify example commands, section structure, and reader-facing terminology.
Sync terminology, decisions, and open questions into the long-term knowledge index.

execution_stages.name: Stage 1 · Task Clarification
execution_stages.goal: Provide fill-in inputs for terminology alignment, decision consolidation, index organization, and question logging.
deliverables: Fill-in conclusions for terminology alignment, decision consolidation, index organization, and question logging
exit_checks: Fill-in inputs are delivered to the owner per the handoff contract.
Fill-in conclusions do not exceed this link body's scope.

execution_stages.name: Stage 2 · Primary Delivery
execution_stages.goal: The Knowledge Link Body produces the main deliverable.
exit_checks: Primary delivery is complete and traceable.
Key risks include rollback paths or alternatives.
Support link bodies have received required fill-in inputs.

execution_stages.name: Stage 3 · Cross-Validation
execution_stages.goal: Rationality Link Body delivers evidence grading, counterevidence search, conflict arbitration, and confidence calibration.
exit_checks: Rationality Link Body outputs are structured and complete.
Risks, boundaries, and next actions are explicit.
Handoff outputs are directly consumable downstream.

execution_stages.name: Stage 4 · Integration Handoff
execution_stages.goal: Knowledge Link Body consolidates terminology, decisions, index, and open questions.
exit_checks: Knowledge Link Body outputs are structured and complete.
Risks, boundaries, and next actions are explicit.
Handoff outputs are directly consumable downstream.

resource_arbitration.summary: Allocate resources for the mission "Consolidate knowledge handoff, terminology index, resource arbitration rules, and README examples to form an OpenClaw collaboration layer" from P0 to P3: close risk gates first, protect the primary chain, then support and deferred work.

priority_slots.objective: Handle high-risk items, evidence conflicts, irreversible actions, and security/permission gates first.
priority_slots.reason: If risk gates are not closed, downstream execution amplifies uncertainty and rework cost.

priority_slots.objective: Keep the Knowledge Link Body primary delivery moving.
priority_slots.reason: The primary chain decides whether this round produces a core deliverable.

priority_slots.objective: Handle integration, runbooks, instructions, and knowledge landing support work.
priority_slots.reason: These directly impact usability, maintainability, and consumption of the deliverable.

priority_slots.objective: Collect deferrable research, optimization, polish, or extension items.
priority_slots.reason: Before higher priorities are cleared, these must not block the main task.

contention_rules: When the P0 risk gate is open, other stages must not preempt its required resources.
When the Knowledge Link Body core delivery conflicts with other support tasks, prioritize P1 primary chain work.
Support work should reuse existing stage outputs and must not recreate the same context.
Deferrable items sink to P3 and are recorded in the knowledge handoff; do not masquerade as blockers.
When test context exists, validation resources must not be preempted by documentation or polish work.
When the Knowledge Link Body scope is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When the Rationality Link Body scope is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When the Validation Link Body scope is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When the Documentation Link Body scope is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When the Security Link Body scope is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
escalation_rules: If P0 and P1 contend for the same input and cannot be split, escalate to the Primary Conductor for arbitration.
When security, evidence, and delivery goals conflict, preserve options that are more verifiable and reversible.
When support work exceeds this round's capacity, move it into knowledge handoff and follow-up plans.
Knowledge Link Body boundary: only handle terminology unification, decision consolidation, index organization, and question logging.
Rationality Link Body boundary: only handle evidence grading, counterevidence search, conflict arbitration, and confidence calibration.
Validation Link Body boundary: only handle reproduction, assertions, evidence collection, and regression validation.
Documentation Link Body boundary: only handle inventory, structure organization, example writing, and proofreading release.
Security Link Body boundary: only handle threat modeling, permission audits, hardening guidance, and compliance closure.
deferred_work: Non-blocking performance tweaks, copy polish, and extra extension points.
Long-term abstractions and large-scale refactors that do not affect current acceptance.
Supplementary retrospectives beyond the knowledge index, captured after the main loop closes.

mode_notes.lite: Do not create subagent agents in OpenClaw; subagent responsibilities are executed inline by the link body.
mode_notes.full: Create all subagent agents in OpenClaw (see openclaw.settings.json for the subagent list).

openclaw_install_plan.summary: Generate an OpenClaw-ready install plan for the mission "Consolidate knowledge handoff, terminology index, resource arbitration rules, and README examples to form an OpenClaw collaboration layer." supporting lite / full modes.

coordination_rules: All support link body outputs must flow back to the controller and the primary link body.
Multi-agent reporting must use the [xx-body]:xxx format.
activation_steps: The controller reads runtime/shared/mission-context.json, runtime/task-board.json, and runtime/topology.json.
The controller dispatches Stage 1 and Stage 2 tasks to the primary link body.
After Stage 2, dispatch cross-validation and support fill-in tasks.
The controller aggregates Stage 3 and support outputs to drive Stage 4 integration handoff.

mode_notes.lite: Do not create subagent agents in OpenClaw; subagent responsibilities are executed inline by the link body.
mode_notes.full: Create all subagent agents in OpenClaw; runtime topology remains link-body level.

primary_link_body.name: Knowledge Link Body
rationality_obligations: All consolidated items must distinguish confirmed facts, temporary hypotheses, and pending verification questions.
All decision records must state selection reasons and rejection reasons.
All reusable entry points must bind to paths, commands, or concrete structures.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: terminology and concept index
Delivery must cover: key decisions and rejection rationale
Delivery must cover: open questions and maintenance list

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Knowledge Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

support_link_bodies.name: Rationality Link Body
rationality_obligations: All key conclusions must attempt to seek counterevidence.
All evidence must be graded; do not treat all evidence equally.
All arbitration decisions must state selection and rejection reasons.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: evidence grading matrix
Delivery must cover: counterevidence and conflict arbitration report
Delivery must cover: confidence calibration conclusion

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Rationality Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

support_link_bodies.name: Validation Link Body
rationality_obligations: All validations must map to explicit assertions.
All failures must provide reproduction steps and localization clues.
Evidence consolidation must trace to specific judgments.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: issue evidence list

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Validation Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

support_link_bodies.name: Documentation Link Body
rationality_obligations: Documentation must match current structure, commands, and outputs.
All examples must be executable or clearly state prerequisites.
Reader-facing conclusions must distinguish implemented vs planned capabilities.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: documentation information architecture
Delivery must cover: user-facing instructions and examples
Delivery must cover: migration or update notes

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Documentation Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

support_link_bodies.name: Security Link Body
rationality_obligations: All security judgments must state threat assumptions and attack paths.
All hardening recommendations must state cost, impact, and priority.
Unverified vulnerabilities must be expressed as risk hypotheses, not confirmed facts.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: threat surface and risk list
Delivery must cover: permission or sensitive information audit conclusions
Delivery must cover: hardening and compliance recommendations

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Security Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

workflow: The Primary Conductor loads the Absolute Rationality Protocol and clarifies knowns, unknowns, assumptions, and acceptance criteria.
Dispatch the primary task to the primary link body: Knowledge Link Body.
In lite mode, do not create subagent agents in OpenClaw; subagent responsibilities are executed inline by the link body to produce the primary delivery.
In full mode, the Knowledge Link Body conductor takes internal orchestration and loads its member subagents.
In full mode, member subagents work in parallel to produce fact extraction, solution construction, risk analysis, validation evidence, and stage reports.
In full mode, the Knowledge Link Body conductor consolidates member outputs into the primary delivery.
Support link bodies (Rationality, Validation, Documentation, Security) run counterevidence, validation, integration, or fill-in tasks in sequence.
Rationality Link Body performs evidence grading, counterevidence search, conflict arbitration, and confidence calibration for all key conclusions.
Knowledge Link Body consolidates terminology, key decisions, reusable entry points, and open questions into a long-term knowledge handoff.
Adjacent stages pass inputs and outputs by the handoff contracts to avoid context loss or orphaned responsibility.
When link bodies or stages contend for resources, the arbitration layer handles risk gates first, then protects the primary delivery.
The Primary Conductor outputs the final conclusions, evidence summary, risk list, confidence level, and next-round actions.

quick_start: Read manifest.json to confirm mode, primary link body, and support chain.
Read the protocols under protocols/ and then agents/00_primary-conductor.md.
Read runtime/topology.json and runtime/task-board.json, then advance via the multi-agent task board.
The Primary Conductor dispatches stage tasks and consolidates returned outputs.
do_not_assume: Do not start without subagents (sessions_spawn) enabled.
Do not skip the task board or routing handoffs.
